repository.name: move-anything-waveform-editor
repository.description: Waveform Editor tool for Move Everything - trim, gain adjust, normalize, and edit WAV files on the Ableton Move

commit.message:
Refactor DSP to stereo, fix drum preset export, and misc improvements

- Refactor DSP from mono int16 to interleaved stereo int16 (L,R,L,R,...)
- Mono WAV files duplicated to both channels on load
- Support PCM 16-bit, PCM 24-bit, and IEEE float 32-bit input
- WAV output always stereo 16-bit PCM
- Waveform display and zero-crossing use L channel
- Fix DSP export symbol: plugin_init -> move_plugin_init_v2
- Fix build.sh to copy from src/dsp/dsp.so (Docker output path)
- Fix drum preset sampleUri: URL-encode spaces (%20)
- Default auto slice threshold from 5 to 25
- Disable loop playback in slice mode, restore on exit
- Back from record-ready state exits module entirely

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix recording race condition and improve recorded file handling

- Add "stopping" state to poll sampler finalization before loading WAV,
  preventing "No waveform data" error from async stop
- Show "SAVING..." screen while waiting for WAV to finalize
- Track recorded files as unsaved (dirty) with discard-on-exit cleanup
- Add timestamp to edit filenames (sample_edit_0306_1423.wav)
- Clean up recording UI header (remove debug VU display)
- Add dirty param support to DSP plugin

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Remap knobs: E4=vertical scale, E5=gain/threshold, E7/E8=twist actions

- E3: zoom only (removed shift+E3 vertical scale)
- E4: vertical scale for all modes (was shift+E3)
- E5: gain/normalize in trim/loop, threshold in slice auto (was E4)
- E7 touch+twist: toggle loop on/off
- E8 touch+twist: snap to zero crossing
- Twist prompts stay visible while knob is touched
- Add snapToZeroCrossing() helper function
- Recording waveform: scrolling write-head visualization
- Recording stop: poll for sampler finalization before loading
- Shift+pad preview: 5% of selection, clamped 0.25s-5s
- Update help.json for new knob layout

fix(visuals): Sync visual data after number dedup, fix ten_frame for addition, add object name to LLM instruction

Three structural bugs fixed:
- Re-compute visual_data when Steps 6b/6c regenerate slot numbers,
  preventing group counts from diverging from a+b values
- Exclude ten_frame for addition/subtraction topics (prefer object_group
  which correctly shows two groups being combined)

  generated question text matches the visual emoji

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Bundle API with esbuild to fix serverless module resolution

Vercel's @vercel/node runtime can't resolve extensionless ESM imports
across directories. Fix by bundling all server code into a single
api/index.js file using esbuild during the build step.

- Move api/index.ts → server/vercel-entry.ts (source)
- Build step: esbuild bundles server code → api/index.js
- api/index.js is .gitignored (build artifact)
- npm packages remain external (available at runtime)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Replace icon-only sidebar collapse with full hide/show toggle

Simplify sidebar from 3-state (expanded/collapsed-60px/hidden) to 2-state
(expanded-260px/fully-hidden-0px). The old 60px icon-only collapse wasted
space and had a hidden toggle button (opacity:0) most users never found.

Now: always-visible "Hide" button at sidebar bottom, small reveal tab at
left edge when hidden, Cmd/Ctrl+\ shortcut unchanged. State persists in
localStorage with flash prevention. Reveal tab hidden in focus mode.

Addresses roadmap item f321325d ("An Expandable Side Bar").

repository.description: Valium — your little helper for getting things done. Inspired by the Rolling Stones' Mother's Little Helper.

commit.message:
Namespace Anthropic model env vars, drop noisy stream debug log

The Anthropic backend was sharing MODEL_SMART/BALANCED/FAST env vars with
DeepInfra, so they'd collide if both backends were configured on the same
machine. Now the Anthropic backend reads ANTHROPIC_MODEL_SMART, etc.
(previous commit did the same for DEEPINFRA_MODEL_*).

Also removed the per-chunk debug log in claude_code_output.stream_response
that was dumping every streaming JSON line — extremely noisy in production.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: docketeer
repository.description: A reasonably sized autonomous AI construction kit

commit.message:
feat: 13 strategy & robustness improvements (Phase 2)

#1  Inventory skew: shift bid/ask to reduce directional exposure
#2  YES/NO coordination: scale down sizing when both sides held
#3  Dynamic spread: volatility + depth premium via sigma of snapshots
#4  Volatility estimation: stddev of mid prices, passed in OrderBookUpdate
#5  Jump detection: 5% move triggers configurable cooldown per market
#6  Intelligent requoting: HOLD is now skew & liquidation-mode aware
#7  Basket exposure: cluster limit via MM_CLUSTERS + MM_MAX_CLUSTER_USD
#8  Position age: liquidation mode (ASK-only) for positions > 24h
#9  Fill quality: score fills after 5 min vs mid price (adverse selection)
#10 Monitoring metrics: /api/metrics endpoint + dashboard section
#11 Queue priority: post 1 tick inside best bid/ask when profitable
#12 Circuit breaker: error categories (NETWORK/RATE_LIMIT/COLLATERAL/CLOB)
    + per-market 5-min cooldown after repeated CLOB errors
#13 Liveness monitoring: alert if no orders placed > MM_LIVENESS_ORDER_MINUTES

Config additions: MM_DYNAMIC_SPREAD, MM_INVENTORY_SKEW_FACTOR,
MM_JUMP_THRESHOLD, MM_JUMP_COOLDOWN_SECONDS, MM_MAX_POSITION_AGE_HOURS,
MM_CLUSTERS, MM_MAX_CLUSTER_USD, MM_LIVENESS_ORDER_MINUTES

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Namespace model env vars per backend and drop noisy stream debug log

The MODEL_SMART/BALANCED/FAST env vars were shared across backends, so
switching between Anthropic and DeepInfra meant the same variables could
collide. Now each backend owns its own: DOCKETEER_ANTHROPIC_MODEL_SMART
and DOCKETEER_DEEPINFRA_MODEL_SMART, etc.

Also removed the per-line debug log in claude_code_output.stream_response
that was dumping every streaming JSON chunk to the log.

fix: allow Ctrl+Tab/Ctrl+Shift+Tab to cycle tabs when terminal is active

attachCustomKeyEventHandler to let Ctrl+Tab bubble up.

commit.message:
V2: date-based match fetch + UpcomingMatches admin page

- Replace series import flow with simple date-range fetch from Sportmonks
- New predMatchFetchService: fetches fixtures by date, upserts teams/players/matches
- New UpcomingMatches.tsx: date picker (3-day window), Fetch button, match cards
- Match cards show team names, type, venue, time, league, player counts + warning if low
- Create Contest button per match → auto-generates draft → navigate to editor

commit.message:
fix: parse CDATA-wrapped sitemap URLs (AIOSEO Pro compatibility)

AIOSEO Pro wraps all sitemap values in CDATA sections, e.g.
<loc><![CDATA[https://...]]></loc>, causing 0 matches with the
previous regex. Updated loc/lastmod parsing to handle both plain
and CDATA-wrapped values.

commit.message:
Fix exit offer crash in MY_APP mode by propagating purchase handlers (#6391)

When `purchasesAreCompletedBy` is set to `.myApp`, the PaywallView
requires `performPurchase` and `performRestore` handlers to be provided.
The exit offer PaywallViewController was being created with `nil` for
both handlers, causing a fatal error when the user dismissed the paywall
and an exit offer was configured.

This change captures the parent controller's handlers from its
PurchaseHandler and passes them through to the exit offer controller,
following the same pattern already used for delegate, dismiss handler,
fonts, and touch blocking configuration.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
iter35b: GPU continuous enforcement — per-call tok/s in run_once.py + fix probe interval

run_once.py:
- _i35_percall_toks: module-level list accumulates tok/s for every _translate_event_bullets_1to1 call
- _translate_event_bullets_1to1: time each HTTP call (monotonic), extract usage.completion_tokens,
  compute tok_s; if 0 < tok_s < 12 → write _gpu_fallback_detected.flag + return GPU_FALLBACK_DETECTED immediately
- _translate_digest_1to1: propagate GPU_FALLBACK_DETECTED without retry (abort immediately)
- _write_translation_engine_meta: add calls_tok_s/tok_s_min/avg/max/cpu_fallback_detected/gpu_required
  from _i35_percall_toks global accumulator

verify_online.ps1:
- probe interval 60s → 120s (spec requirement)
- fix corrupted Chinese string on GPU_CONTINUOUS_ENFORCEMENT_HARD startup line
- patching block: prefer run_once.py per-call calls_tok_s; probe history → probe_tok_s_history key
- cpu_fallback_detected: OR of flag file + run_once.py per-call check

commit.message:
fix(database): correct integrations and instagram_posts RLS policies (#190) (#234)

* fix(database): correct integrations and instagram_posts RLS policies (#190)

professional_id references professionals.id, not auth.users.id, so
`professional_id = auth.uid()` always returned false. Changed to use
subquery `professional_id IN (SELECT id FROM professionals WHERE user_id = auth.uid())`.

commit.message:
fix: seed bundled custom EEP profiles on startup (v2.1.5)

Bundled manufacturer profiles (e.g., Kessel Staufix A5-30-03) were not
loaded because they lived in /app/data/custom_eep/ while the EEP manager
only searched /data/custom_eep/. Now on startup, bundled profiles are
auto-seeded to persistent storage. If the bundled version has more fields
than an existing profile, it auto-updates (fixes wrong standard offsets).
